zh.lifeSummary: 當Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël於大約1175年出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉蒙特福吉素-時，他的父親Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé31歲，母親Gervaise de Saye24歲。他於1198年與Domette de Sille le Guillaume在法國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1238年之後在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël于大约1175年出生在法国布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉蒙特福吉素-时，他的父亲Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé31岁，母亲Gervaise de Saye24岁。他于1198年与Domette de Sille le Guillaume在法国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1238年之后在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gael
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, Welsh, and Irish: nickname from Celtic gall ‘foreigner, stranger’, a word found in Irish, Gaelic, and Breton. In the Scottish Highlands the Gaelic term gall was applied to people from the English-speaking lowlands and to Scandinavians; in Ireland the same term was applied to settlers who arrived from Wales and England in the wake of the Anglo-Norman invasion of the 12th century. This surname is also found at an early date in Lincolnshire, where it apparently has a Breton origin (compare 4 below and Legall ), having been introduced by Breton followers of the Norman Conquerors.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Gale  .

French, German, Danish, Polish, and Czech; Slovak (mainly Gáll); Hungarian (Gáll): from the Latin personal name Gallus, originally a Roman surname meaning ‘Gaul’ or ‘rooster’ (see Gallo 2). The name was widespread in Europe during the Middle Ages, in central Europe largelly due to the cult of a 7th-century Irish monk and missionary, Saint Gall, whose name, Latinized as Gallus, is presumably of Celtic origin (see 1 above). Among other things, Saint Gall established a Christian settlement to the south of Lake Constance, which became the monastery later known as St. Gallen. The Latin(ized) name Gallus was taken into Czech as Havel , into Polish as Gaweł (see Gawel ), into Slovak and Hungarian as Gál (see Gal ), and into Slovenian and Croatian as Gal .

variants.name: Galbraith
meanings: Scottish: ethnic name for a Strathclyde Briton, from Gaelic gall Breathnach ‘British foreigner’. Compare Gall .

History: This name is first recorded as a surname in the Lennox, a rich agricultural region north of Glasgow under the Campsie Fells, in the 12th century. In early medieval times the region to which Lennox belongs was an independent Welsh-speaking kingdom, with its capital at Dumbarton. It was not integrated into the rest of Scotland until 1124. The first recorded chief of the Galbraiths was Gilchrist ‘the Briton’, living in 1193.

variants.name: Gal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Aharon, Eti, Ofer, Aviva, Dorit, Dov, Gadi, Moshe, Noam, Ronen.  Hungarian Imre, Sandor, Janos, Laszlo, Bela, Ildiko.  Polish Andrej, Dariusz, Feliks, Kazimierz, Stanislaw, Wlodzimierz.

Hungarian, Slovak, and Czech (Gál); Polish (also Gał); Rusyn (in Slovakia also Gál, in Poland also Gał); Slovenian and Croatian: from the personal name Hungarian, Slovak, and Czech Gál, Polish, Rusyn, Slovenian, and Croatian Gal, from Latin Gallus (see Gall 3), the name borne by a 7th-century Irish missionary who was very active in central Europe and is venerated as a saint in the Catholic Church. This personal name was widespread in Europe in the Middle Ages, and it gave rise to many surnames, e.g. Slovak Galko, Galčík, Galik , Gall , Gallik , Galovič (see Galovic ), and Galus , and Slovenian Gale . In North America, the surname Gal could also be a shortening of some of them.
Croatian and Slovenian: from the old personal name Gal, from gal, an obsolete word meaning ‘black’.

variants.name: Gallman
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German Gallmann: variant of Gall 3, with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Galler
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Fritz, Otto, Reinhold.  Jewish Hyman, Emanuel, Meyer, Myer, Naum.
German: patronymic from a personal name (from Latin Gallus) which was widespread in Europe in the Middle Ages (see Gall 3).
German: nickname for someone in the service of the monastery of St. Gallen (see   Gall   3), or a habitational name for someone from the city in Switzerland so named.

variants.name: Galiano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Blanca, Jaime, Jorge, Juan, Julio, Pedro.  Italian Dino, Eduardo, Rolando, Adolfo, Aida, Angelo, Fausto, Gennaro, Luciano, Nunzio, Rocco.
Spanish and southern Italian: from the personal name Galiano, from the Late Latin name Gallianus, a derivative of Gallius, from Gallus (see Gall 3). Compare Italian Galliano .
Italian: habitational name from a place called Galiano.

variants.name: Gallet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Alain, Gilberte, Michel, Pierre, Vernice, Yves.
French:
nickname for a cheerful companion, a noun derivative of the Old French verb gal(l)er ‘to enjoy oneself, to have fun’.

variants.name: Legall
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Yves, Aliette, Francois, Jean Luc, Michel, Mireille.

Breton (mainly Finistère and Côtes-d'Armor; mainly Le Gall): nickname for a French-speaking person in Brittany, from Breton Gall ‘Gaulish person, Frenchman’, with the French masculine definite article le. See also Gall 4.
German: variant of   Legel   ‘cooper’ and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Galle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Amie, Colette, Yves.
German: variant of Gall , or a nickname for an evil person, from Middle High German galle ‘bile, bitterness, falsehood’.
Flemish: from the personal name Galle, a Romance or French form of ancient Germanic Wal(l)o ‘man from Wallonia’.

variants.name: Galliano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Enrico, Geno.  French Alain, Monique.

Italian (mainly Piedmont): from the personal name Galliano, from the Late Latin name Gallianus, a derivative of Gallius, from Gallus (see Gall 3). This surname is also found in southern France. Compare Galiano .

variants.name: Galewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Galew, two villages in Greater Poland Voivodeship, or Galewice in Łódź Voivodeship. See also   Gall   3.

nameMeanings.name: Montfort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Breton: variant of   Monfort   1. This surname is most common in Brittany and Haute-Savoie. See also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort: habitational name from Montfoort in Utrecht. It is a cognate of 1 above (see Monfort 1) and also its altered form (or perhaps vice versa).
English (of Norman origin): very rare variant of   Mountford  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Monfort
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Wilner.

French and Breton: habitational name from any of numerous places called Montfort, from Old French mont ‘hill’ (see Mont 1) + fort ‘strong, impregnable’ (from Latin fortis). This surname is by far most common in Brittany. It is also found in Haiti. Compare Montfort 1, see also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort, a variant of Montfort 2 and also a cognate of the French name in 1 above and an altered form of its variant Montfort 1 (or perhaps vice versa).

variants.name: Monforte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmela, Carmine, Rocco, Rosaria, Sal.  Spanish Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Medardo.

Italian and Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Monforte, from Latin mons ‘mountain’ (genitive montis) + fortis ‘strong’, probably a reference to the strategic position of these locations.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Montfort  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Raoul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See Ralph .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël was born about 1175, in Montfort-sur-Meu, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, his father, Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé, was 31 and his mother, Gervaise de Saye, was 24. He married Domette de Sille le Guillaume in 1198, in France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died after 1238, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël est né environ 1175, à Montfort-sur-Meu, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, son père, Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Gervaise de Saye, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Domette de Sille le Guillaume en 1198, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé après 1238, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël um 1175 in Montfort-sur-Meu, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé 31 und seine Mutter Gervaise de Saye 24. Er hat 1198 in Frankreich Domette de Sille le Guillaume geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1238 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël è nato il circa 1175 nel Montfort-sur-Meu, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Gervaise de Saye, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Domette de Sille le Guillaume nel 1198, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1238, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaëlが約1175年にフランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインモントフォート-スル-メウで生まれた時，父Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédéは31歳，母Gervaise de Sayeは24歳であった。1198年にフランスでDomette de Sille le Guillaumeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1238年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël(이)가 대략 1175년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 몽포트-수-우에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Gervaise de Saye(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1198년 프랑스에서 Domette de Sille le Guillaume(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1238년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël nasceu aproximadamente 1175, em Montfort-sur-Meu, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Gervaise de Saye, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Domette de Sille le Guillaume em 1198, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu após 1238, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël родился около 1175 г. в местности монтфорт-сур-меу, илле-ет-вилеин, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Gervaise de Saye, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Domette de Sille le Guillaume в 1198 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер после 1238 г. в его родном городе.

sm.Abt 1175–Aft 1238: e tusa o le 1175–ina ua ma'ea le 1238

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Raoul IV de Gael Montfort Chevalier banneret, seigneur de Gaël nació aproximadamente 1175, en Montfort-sur-Meu, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Geoffroy I de Montfort-le-Hédé, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Gervaise de Saye, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Domette de Sille le Guillaume en 1198, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1238, en su pueblo.
